hyperedges.description: Three instrumentation layers (HTTP middleware, MySQL driver hooks, Redis client hooks) all feed into MetricMonitor's Prometheus metrics, which are consumed by three Grafana dashboards.
The Wrap function produces a Driver that wraps Conn, Stmt, and DriveTx with Hook callbacks (Before/After/OnError), forming a transparent instrumentation decorator chain for database/sql.

Build script compiles services into program directory; start_node_exporter launches webapp alongside node_exporter; system dashboard consumes node_exporter metrics while app dashboards consume the custom Prometheus metrics exposed by MetricMonitor.

hyperedges.label: ELK Logging Pipeline
hyperedges.description: Filebeat collects logs via logconf inputs, ships to Logstash for processing, which forwards to Elasticsearch for storage. Kibana provides visualization on top of Elasticsearch.

hyperedges.label: Prometheus-Grafana Monitoring Stack
hyperedges.description: Prometheus scrapes metrics from node-exporter (system metrics on mynode:9100) and webapp (custom metrics on mynode:8090). Grafana provides dashboard visualization backed by Prometheus data. Both run as Docker services orchestrated via docker-compose.

hyperedges.label: OpenSpec OPSX Change Workflow Pipeline
hyperedges.description: A complete change lifecycle: opsx:new creates scaffold, opsx:continue/propose/ff build artifacts in dependency order, opsx:apply implements tasks, opsx:verify validates correctness, opsx:archive preserves the change record. opsx:sync handles delta spec merging and opsx:bulk-archive batches multiple archives. opsx:explore and opsx:onboard are orthogonal — explore is a thinking mode, onboard is a tutorial.

hyperedges.label: OpenSpec Change Workflow Pipeline
hyperedges.description: The core OpenSpec workflow forms a pipeline: new-change starts a change container, continue-change fills artifacts in sequence (proposal -> specs -> design -> tasks), apply-change implements the tasks, and archive-change finalizes. propose and ff are shortcuts that create all artifacts at once. explore is a cross-cutting thinking mode. verify validates before archive. sync-specs propagates delta specs to main specs.

hyperedges.label: RepoMind Knowledge Lifecycle
hyperedges.description: The RepoMind knowledge lifecycle: init rebuilds the knowledge graph and seeds concepts/modules, prd extracts business concepts from historical PRD documents, query routes questions through the knowledge base before answering, and summary runs a post-answer gate to persist reusable findings back into the knowledge base. Each phase feeds the next, with query->summary being a synchronous blocking step.

nodes.label: SMB-oriented monitoring system design
nodes.rationale: Design goal: build a monitoring system for small/medium businesses that can be directly copied (复刻) into their projects. Covers metric design, software config, monitoring solutions. Uses Prometheus+Grafana stack with custom instrumentation via driver wrapping and middleware hooks.

nodes.label: Slow query detection (>1s) and long transaction detection (>8s)
nodes.rationale: HookDb.After checks if query duration >= 1 second to flag slow queries (log level ERROR). DriveTx.Commit/Rollback checks if transaction duration > 8 seconds to flag long transactions. These thresholds are hardcoded.
nodes.norm_label: slow query detection (>1s) and long transaction detection (>8s)

nodes.label: Shard table name normalization for metric aggregation
nodes.rationale: SqlMonitor.SetFixName allows custom table name normalization so that sharded tables (e.g., t_user_001, t_user_002) are aggregated under a canonical name (t_user) for monitoring purposes. This prevents metric explosion from sharded tables.
nodes.norm_label: shard table name normalization for metric aggregation

nodes.label: Multi-table select query warning
nodes.rationale: HookDb.After logs a warning (log level WARN) when a SELECT query involves 2+ tables and is not a slow query. This detects potentially expensive multi-table joins as a database performance concern.

nodes.label: Transparent instrumentation via driver wrapping
nodes.rationale: The Hooks/Wrap pattern wraps database/sql/driver.Driver to inject Before/After/OnError callbacks transparently. This allows monitoring without modifying application SQL code. The same pattern is used for Redis via WrapProcess/WrapProcessPipeline.

nodes.label: Pluggable alert notification strategy
nodes.rationale: alerterserver accepts alert log/grafana webhooks but currently only prints them. TODO comments indicate the intent to add pluggable strategies (e.g., DingTalk group notifications) based on app service name and alert content.

nodes.label: OpenSpec Artifact-Driven Workflow Model
nodes.rationale: Uses spec-driven schema where each change produces ordered artifacts: proposal → specs → design → tasks. Commands query openspec status --json to determine artifact readiness based on dependency satisfaction. The workflow is fluid — actions on a change can be invoked anytime, not phase-locked.

nodes.label: OpenSpec Change Lifecycle Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Changes flow through a pipeline: new (scaffold) → continue/propose/ff (create artifacts) → apply (implement) → verify (validate) → archive (preserve). Explore mode exists orthogonal to the pipeline for ad-hoc investigation. Sync handles delta-to-main spec merging independently. Bulk-archive batches the archive step for multiple changes with conflict resolution.

nodes.label: Summary Gate
nodes.rationale: Summary gate must be synchronous and blocking — the agent cannot respond to the user until the gate completes. This prevents knowledge drift where the agent moves on before findings are persisted.

nodes.label: Frontmatter Metadata Routing
nodes.rationale: RepoMind uses per-document frontmatter (name/description/keywords) for first-round routing instead of a centralized index.json or directory README. The description field is the primary index summary; modules also maintain keywords as auxiliary locators. This design avoids a single point of routing failure and allows each document to self-describe its retrieval relevance.

nodes.label: Explore as Stance Not Workflow
nodes.rationale: Explore mode is a stance (curious, adaptive, patient), not a fixed workflow with required steps. It forbids implementation but allows reading code and creating artifacts. This design enables free-form thinking without the pressure of producing specific outputs.

nodes.author: openspec
nodes.norm_label: openspec-apply-change: implement tasks from an openspec change

nodes.label: openspec-continue-change: Continue working on a change by creating the next artifact

nodes.author: openspec

nodes.author: openspec
nodes.norm_label: openspec-onboard: guided onboarding walkthrough for openspec

nodes.label: openspec-propose: Create change and all artifacts in one step
nodes.author: openspec
nodes.norm_label: openspec-propose: create change and all artifacts in one step

nodes.label: openspec-sync-specs: Sync delta specs from a change to main specs
nodes.author: openspec
nodes.norm_label: openspec-sync-specs: sync delta specs from a change to main specs

nodes.label: openspec-verify-change: Verify implementation matches change artifacts
nodes.author: openspec

nodes.norm_label: repomind-prd: extract business concepts from historical prd documents

nodes.norm_label: repomind-query: route business/code questions through repomind knowledge before answering

nodes.norm_label: repomind-summary: post-answer summary gate for persisting reusable findings

nodes.author: openspec
nodes.norm_label: openspec change: a container for work holding proposal, specs, design, and tasks artifacts
